title: Regulatory consultation trigger monitoring

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for Art.23.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that APPI Art.23 (Security control action) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.29.23: Regulatory consultation trigger monitoring.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.29.23' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails, DLP system outputs, consent management platforms, data classification tools, cross-border transfer logs, privacy impact assessments, breach notification records
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=ticketing sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" cat_item="*DPIA*" earliest=-180d
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval closed=strptime(closed_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval age=round((now()-opened)/86400,0)
| where isnull(closed) AND age>60
description: Provides APAC privacy supervisory evidence for regulatory consultation trigger monitoring aligned with APPI; PDPA Thailand.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for APPI compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ticketing and sourcetypes snow:sc_req_item. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Maintain `apac_transfer_register.csv` and consent or DPIA lookups referenced in runbooks. (2) Map technical identifiers to records of processing activities with legal review. (3) Restrict indexes to regional privacy roles with least privilege. (4) Quarterly DPIA and consent linkage review with local counsel. Indexes required: `index=ticketing`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item`.
visualization: Stacked bar (jurisdiction x action), Table (open reviews), Single value (exports without mechanism). Key fields: `age`, `closed`, `opened`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails, DLP system outputs, consent management platforms, data classification tools, cross-border transfer logs, privacy impact assessments, breach notification records.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `apac_transfer_register.csv` and consent or DPIA lookups referenced in runbooks. (2) Map technical identifiers to records of processing activities with legal review. (3) Restrict indexes to regional privacy roles with least privilege. (4) Quarterly DPIA and consent linkage review with local counsel.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ticketing sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" cat_item="*DPIA*" earliest=-180d
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval closed=strptime(closed_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval age=round((now()-opened)/86400,0)
| where isnull(closed) AND age>60
```

Understanding this SPL

**Regulatory consultation trigger monitoring (APPI; PDPA Thailand)** — Provides APAC privacy supervisory evidence for regulatory consultation trigger monitoring aligned with APPI; PDPA Thailand.

Documented **Data sources**: Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails, DLP system outputs, consent management platforms, data classification tools, cross-border transfer logs, privacy impact assessments, breach notification records. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ticketing; **sourcetype**: snow:sc_req_item. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ticketing, sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **opened** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **closed** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(closed) AND age>60` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar (jurisdiction x action), Table (open reviews), Single value (exports without mechanism).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for provides apac privacy supervisory evidence for regulatory consultation trigger monitoring aligned with appi; pdpa thailand. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
